meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve Debugging (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Master Git with AI to conquer debugging. A technical guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your software engineering career.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at stack traces. Integrate AI into your Git workflow to automate root-cause analysis. By using Claude Code to interpret commit diffs and Antigravity for architectural oversight, you transform debugging from a manual struggle into a precise, automated diagnostic process that accelerates your development velocity significantly.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the terminal spits out a cryptic error, and you are stuck in a loop of broken commits. For every student developer, the 'debugging wall' is the primary barrier between a hobbyist and a professional engineer. It is the moment where logic fails and frustration takes over.

But what if your Git history wasn't just a save-point, but a diagnostic engine? By weaving AI directly into your version control, you turn every commit into a data point for an intelligent agent. This workflow doesn't just fix your bugs; it teaches you how to write code that avoids them entirely.

steps.title: Contextualize the Repository
steps.description: Before debugging, you must provide the AI with the full state of your project. This ensures the model understands your file structure and dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current repository structure and identify potential circular dependencies or common anti-patterns in the src directory.

steps.title: Automate Diff Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to isolate the specific commit that introduced the regression, comparing current broken state to the last known good state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the last three commits and highlight the logic change that most likely triggered the current runtime error.

steps.title: Simulate Edge Case Scenarios
steps.description: Generate unit tests based on the bug to ensure the fix is robust and handles unexpected inputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of edge-case tests for the failing module that covers null pointers and type mismatches.

steps.title: Refactor with AI Guidance
steps.description: Apply the fix using an iterative approach, ensuring the codebase remains clean and follows best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified problematic function into a more modular, testable pattern without changing existing interfaces.

steps.title: Validate and Commit
steps.description: Verify the fix against your test suite and generate a professional, descriptive commit message.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a concise, professional commit message based on the changes made to resolve the bug, following conventional commits.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m "fix: resolve memory leak in data processing module"

pro_tips: Always use 'git stash' before allowing AI to perform heavy refactoring so you can revert instantly.
Tag your commits with 'ai-fix' to build a searchable knowledge base of how you solved specific bugs.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command on every pull request to catch logic errors before they hit main.
Feed your error logs into Antigravity as a raw text file to get a prioritized list of likely culprits.
Never blindly accept AI code; ask the agent to explain the 'why' behind the fix to deepen your own understanding.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your first module, begin documenting your process in a README.md file to showcase your problem-solving methodology to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical arsenal to debug like a senior engineer. Stop letting errors block your momentum and start building with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
